content.text:
Task: Draft a Rust unit test for a NEW module `src/time_format.rs` that does not exist yet. The test must compile-fail (red) because the module, its public types, and its functions do not exist yet — that compile error IS the expected "red" for a fresh test in Rust per this repo's AGENTS.md. Do NOT stub or scaffold any production code in `src/time_format.rs`; the worker will write the production code. Your job is only the test.

The new module will be wired into `src/main.rs` later (don't add the `mod` declaration now — keep the test red for the right reason: the test imports a path that doesn't exist yet).

## What the new module must expose (the contract)

```rust
// src/time_format.rs
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub enum TimeFormat { AmPm, H24 }

pub fn get() -> TimeFormat;
pub fn set(mode: TimeFormat);
pub fn format_time(ms: i64, mode: TimeFormat) -> String;
```

Behavior contract:
- Default value (no saved file) is `TimeFormat::AmPm`.
- `set(AmPm)` then `get() == AmPm`. `set(H24)` then `get() == H24`. Round-trip works in both directions.
- A corrupted/garbage file content falls back to `TimeFormat::AmPm` (resilient, like `text_scale::get()`).
- `format_time` formats Unix-epoch MILLISECONDS (matching the existing `src/ui/mod.rs::fmt_time` signature) as a local-time string (uses `glib::DateTime::from_unix_local` under the hood — do NOT test against a specific timezone; use `glib::DateTime::from_unix_local(ms / 1000).format(...)` so the test is timezone-portable).
- AM/PM format: `%l:%M %p` → e.g. "1:30 PM", "12:00 AM", "12:00 PM", "1:00 AM".
- 24-hour format: `%H:%M` → e.g. "13:30", "00:00", "12:00", "01:00".

## Concrete test cases the test MUST assert

For `format_time`, use specific Unix-epoch-millisecond inputs that map to the local time you want. To avoid hard-coding timezone-dependent timestamps, build the timestamp via `glib::DateTime::new(&glib::TimeZone::local(), year, month, day, hour, minute, 0.0).and_then(|dt| dt.to_unix()).unwrap() * 1000` to get an unambiguous `ms` for the test. This pattern is timezone-portable.

Required assertions (each as a separate `#[test]`):

1. `format_time_24h_afternoon`: `format_time(<13:30 local>, H24) == "13:30"`
2. `format_time_24h_midnight`: `format_time(<00:00 local>, H24) == "00:00"`
3. `format_time_24h_noon`: `format_time(<12:00 local>, H24) == "12:00"`
4. `format_time_ampm_afternoon`: `format_time(<13:30 local>, AmPm) == "1:30 PM"`
5. `format_time_ampm_midnight`: `format_time(<00:00 local>, AmPm) == "12:00 AM"`
6. `format_time_ampm_noon`: `format_time(<12:00 local>, AmPm) == "12:00 PM"`
7. `format_time_ampm_morning`: `format_time(<01:00 local>, AmPm) == "1:00 AM"`
8. `default_is_ampm`: with no saved state file, `get() == TimeFormat::AmPm`
9. `round_trip_ampm`: `set(AmPm); get() == AmPm`
10. `round_trip_h24`: `set(H24); get() == H24`
11. `corrupted_file_falls_back_to_ampm`: write garbage to the state file, `get() == AmPm`

## State-file isolation

`time_format` will use `glib::user_data_dir().join("openbubbles-gtk").join("time_format.txt")` (mirror of `text_scale.rs` exactly). GLib's `g_get_user_data_dir` honors `XDG_DATA_HOME` and caches on first call, so you must set `XDG_DATA_HOME` ONCE at the top of the test module, BEFORE any call to `glib::user_data_dir()` (including by anything that runs first in the test binary).

Use `std::env::set_var("XDG_DATA_HOME", ...)` (safe in edition 2021 — this repo is edition 2021 per Cargo.toml). Per AGENTS.md, "never set XDG_DATA_HOME to redirect paths in a test" is about parallel test threads racing; mitigate by doing ALL the assertions in this single test module (one binary's tests, all single-threaded) and setting `XDG_DATA_HOME` exactly once at the start. Place a single `static INIT: std::sync::Once = std::sync::Once::new();` (or equivalent) at the top of the test module and call it from a `set_up()` helper that every test calls first.

Use a per-process temp dir (e.g. `std::env::temp_dir().join(format!("openbubbles-gtk-timeformat-test-{}", std::process::id()))`) and create it in the init helper. The state file lives at `XDG_DATA_HOME/openbubbles-gtk/time_format.txt`.

## Confirm red

Run `cargo test time_format` from `/work`. The build must fail with an error that mentions `time_format` (or its types `TimeFormat`, `get`, `set`, `format_time`) being unresolvable. That is the right kind of red: a missing production module/symbol, not a typo in the test. If the build fails for any other reason, fix the test and re-run.

## What to put in the test file

Put the test in `src/time_format.rs` at the BOTTOM inside a `#[cfg(test)] mod tests { ... }` (yes, inline in the same file as the future production code — this is the repo's pattern, see `src/store/mod.rs` for an example). The test must `use super::*;` so it pulls from the new module's namespace.

## Report

After writing and confirming red, return a fenced block with:
- `test_files`: paths
- `verify_command`: `cargo test time_format` (run from /work)
- `red_confirmed`: `compile-error` or `assertion-failure` plus the failing reason in one line
- `asserts`: one line per case you asserted, with the expected value
- `contract`: the exact signatures the worker must satisfy
- `notes`: any cases deliberately left out of scope, or `none`

Do not implement production code. Do not edit `src/main.rs`. Do not edit any other file. Read /work/src/text_scale.rs and /work/src/store/mod.rs to mirror their test style.

---
**Output:** Write your findings to: /tmp/time-format-test-draft.md
